mapDescriptions.name: Map descriptions to components
mapDescriptions.description: Write a function that takes a weather description string and returns the list of drawing components for that weather type.

sunny.name: Sunny
sunny.description: Sunny days only need a sun.

miserable.name: Miserable
miserable.description: Miserable days need clouds and rain.

hopeful.name: Hopeful
hopeful.description: Hopeful days need sun and cloud.

rainbowTerritory.name: Rainbow Territory
rainbowTerritory.description: Rainbow territory needs sun, cloud, and rain.

exciting.name: Exciting
exciting.description: Exciting days need cloud and snow.

snowboardingTime.name: Snowboarding Time
snowboardingTime.description: Snowboarding time needs sun, cloud, and snow.

chooseList.question: How do I decide which list to return?
chooseList.answer: Use `if` and `else if` to match the description against each possible weather word. Each branch returns a different list.

branchReturns.question: What does each branch return?
branchReturns.answer: A list of the component strings that make up that weather. For example, sunny might be `["sun"]` while rainy might be `["cloud", "rain"]`.

createList.question: How do I create a list?
createList.answer: Square brackets with comma-separated items, like `["cloud", "rain"]`. Each item needs quotation marks since they're strings.
